summary: generic_html extract_text double-counts nested main>article containers
description: extract_text collects soup.find_all('main') + find_all('article') into one container list. When <article> nests inside <main> (Global Grey page layout), both are collected and their text concatenated — entire book text duplicated (transcendental-magic raw: 1.43M chars from a 760KB page; every chapter heading appears exactly 2x). The id()-based 'seen' dedup only catches the SAME element, not nesting. The 787681d2 first-tier-wins fix addressed class-tier stacking, not intra-tier nesting. Fix: drop any collected container that is a descendant of another collected container. Existing sources unaffected (Gutenberg/sacred-texts/gnosis pages have no main/article nesting — pistis-sophia char ratio is normal) but re-verify checksums stay stable.

resolution.note: Filter containers whose ancestor is also collected. Re-acquired transcendental-magic: 715,448 chars (was 1,433,209), all headings now at correct counts. Sibling-container behaviour covered by test_sibling_containers_both_kept; full downloader suite 11 passed.
